title: Residential DWV Drain Line

components.name: 4" PVC main building drain
Horizontal drain pipe sloped at 1/16" per foot
components.violation: Drain pipes 3" and larger require a minimum slope of 1/8" per foot. Pipes smaller than 3" require 1/4" per foot minimum. A slope of 1/16" per foot will not provide adequate flow velocity to carry solids.

components.name: Cleanout at base of stack
3" branch drain connected with sanitary tee on its back (horizontal)
components.violation: Sanitary tees may only be used for vertical-to-horizontal transitions. For horizontal-to-horizontal connections, a wye with 45-degree bend or combination wye-bend must be used to maintain proper flow.

components.name: Proper pipe support hangers at 4-foot intervals
ABS to PVC connection with solvent cement
components.violation: ABS and PVC cannot be joined with solvent cement. These dissimilar plastics require a listed mechanical coupling (such as a banded no-hub coupling) or approved transition fitting.

explanation: DWV systems must maintain proper slope for drainage, use correct fittings for each orientation, and never mix plastic pipe materials without approved transition fittings. These are fundamental plumbing code requirements.

title: Kitchen Sink Drain Installation

components.name: Basket strainer with proper putty seal
Continuous waste connecting double bowl
S-trap under sink
components.violation: S-traps are prohibited by code because they are prone to self-siphoning, which breaks the trap seal and allows sewer gases into the living space. A P-trap must be used instead.

components.name: 1-1/4" tailpiece from sink
components.violation: Kitchen sinks require a minimum 1-1/2" drain. The 1-1/4" size is only permitted for lavatory (bathroom) sinks. An undersized kitchen drain will clog frequently from food waste.

components.name: Dishwasher drain connected to disposal
AAV (Air Admittance Valve) properly installed

explanation: Kitchen sink plumbing requires P-traps (never S-traps), properly sized drain pipes, and correct connections. S-traps are one of the most common code violations found in DIY plumbing work.

title: Water Heater Installation

components.name: Gas water heater on garage floor
components.violation: Gas-fired water heaters in garages must have their ignition source elevated a minimum of 18 inches above the garage floor to prevent ignition of flammable vapors from gasoline and other fuels.

components.name: T&P relief valve installed in top 6 inches
T&P discharge pipe reduced from 3/4" to 1/2"
components.violation: The T&P relief valve discharge pipe must not be smaller than the valve outlet, which is 3/4". Reducing the pipe restricts the relief flow and creates a dangerous pressure condition.

components.name: Gas shutoff valve within 6 feet of unit
Proper venting through roof
Sediment trap (drip leg) on gas line

explanation: Water heater installations are one of the most inspected plumbing items. Elevation in garages, proper T&P valve piping, and gas safety are all life-safety requirements that protect occupants.

title: Bathroom Lavatory Rough-In

components.name: 1-1/4" P-trap installed correctly
Trap arm longer than allowed distance to vent
components.violation: For a 1-1/4" trap arm, the maximum distance to the vent is 5 feet. Exceeding this distance risks siphoning the trap seal. The trap arm distance is measured from the trap weir to the vent connection.

components.name: Hot water on left side supply
Supply valves (angle stops) installed
Vent pipe connected below the trap weir
components.violation: The vent connection must be above the trap weir (the overflow point of the trap). Connecting below the weir makes it a wet vent carrying waste, not a true vent, and will not properly protect the trap seal.

explanation: Lavatory rough-ins require proper trap arm length to maintain the trap seal and correct vent placement above the trap weir. These details are critical during rough-in inspection.

title: Residential Water Supply Piping

components.name: 3/4" copper main supply entering house
Main shutoff valve at point of entry
No backflow preventer at hose bibb
components.violation: All hose bibbs (sill cocks) must have a backflow preventer or be a listed frost-proof hose bibb with integral vacuum breaker to prevent backflow contamination of the potable water supply.

components.name: 1/2" branch lines to fixtures
Lead solder used on copper joints
components.violation: Lead solder is prohibited on any pipe or fitting in a potable water system. Only lead-free solder (0.2% maximum lead content) may be used per the Safe Drinking Water Act and plumbing codes.

components.name: Water hammer arrestors at washing machine

explanation: Potable water systems must prevent backflow contamination and use only lead-free materials. These requirements protect public health and are strictly enforced.

title: Commercial Restroom Fixture Venting

components.name: Water closets with proper flush valves
3" vent pipe through roof
Crown vent installed on P-trap
components.violation: Crown venting (connecting the vent at the top of the trap) is prohibited because it creates a cross-connection path and can siphon waste into the vent. The vent must connect to the trap arm downstream of the trap.

components.name: Horizontal vent pipe below flood-level rim of fixture
components.violation: Vent pipes must rise vertically to a point at least 6 inches above the flood-level rim of the highest fixture served before turning horizontal. This prevents waste from backing up into the vent.

components.name: Floor drains with trap primers
Urinals with proper spacing and carriers

explanation: Commercial venting systems must follow strict rules about vent connections and routing. Crown venting and improperly routed horizontal vents are common violations that compromise the entire DWV system.

title: Cross-Connection Control

components.name: Backflow preventer on irrigation system
Boiler fill with RPZ backflow preventer
Garden hose submerged in swimming pool
components.violation: A garden hose submerged in a pool creates a direct cross-connection. If a back-siphonage event occurs, contaminated pool water can be drawn into the potable supply. Hose bibbs must have vacuum breakers and hoses must never be submerged.

components.name: Laundry tub faucet with spout below rim
components.violation: Faucets and fill valves must maintain an air gap of at least 1 inch or two pipe diameters (whichever is greater) above the flood-level rim of the fixture. A spout that can be submerged creates a backflow risk.

components.name: Atmospheric vacuum breaker on lawn sprinkler
Water softener with listed air gap on drain

explanation: Cross-connection control is one of the most important aspects of plumbing. Every connection between potable and non-potable water must have appropriate backflow protection to prevent contamination.

title: Gas Piping for Water Heater

components.name: CSST (corrugated stainless steel tubing) with proper bonding
Manual gas shutoff valve within reach
No sediment trap (drip leg) before gas valve
components.violation: A sediment trap (drip leg) must be installed as close as practical to the appliance inlet and ahead of the gas valve. It captures moisture and debris that could damage the gas valve.

components.name: Gas pipe running through a return air duct
components.violation: Gas piping must not be installed in or through any air duct, clothes chute, chimney, vent, dumbwaiter, or elevator shaft. A leak inside a duct would distribute gas throughout the building.

components.name: Proper pipe support at 6-foot intervals

explanation: Gas piping installation requires sediment traps to protect appliance gas valves and must never run through air ducts or concealed shafts where leaks cannot be detected and could spread through the building.

title: Shower/Tub Drain and Valve

components.name: 2" shower drain with strainer
Shower valve without pressure-balancing or thermostatic protection
components.violation: All shower and tub/shower combination valves must be pressure-balancing, thermostatic-mixing, or combination type to prevent scalding. This is a life-safety requirement, especially for children and elderly.

components.name: Shower pan liner with proper dam at door
P-trap installed correctly below drain
Shower head at 80 inches above floor
Hot water supply connected to right side of valve
components.violation: Hot water must be connected to the left side of the valve (when facing the valve from inside the shower). This is a universal plumbing convention and code requirement to prevent scalding confusion.

explanation: Shower installations require anti-scald protection and proper hot/cold orientation. Scalding injuries are serious, and pressure-balancing or thermostatic valves are required by code to protect occupants.

title: Water Service Entry

components.name: 1" copper water service from meter
Main shutoff valve inside building
No expansion tank on closed system
components.violation: When a backflow preventer or check valve is installed on the water service (creating a closed system), a thermal expansion tank is required. Without it, heated water expanding has nowhere to go and can cause dangerous pressure buildup.

components.name: Pressure reducing valve set to 80 PSI
components.violation: Water pressure must not exceed 80 PSI at any fixture. However, pressure reducing valves should typically be set to 60-70 PSI to provide a safety margin. At 80 PSI exactly, there is no margin for pressure spikes or regulator drift.

components.name: Copper pipe properly sleeved through foundation
Dielectric union at copper-to-steel transition

explanation: Water service entry requires proper pressure control and thermal expansion protection. A closed system (with backflow preventer) without an expansion tank can cause the T&P valve on the water heater to weep or, worse, cause pipe or fitting failure.

title: Medical Gas Piping System

components.name: Brazed copper tube joints with nitrogen purge
Pipe labeled with proper color coding
Zone valve boxes properly located
Standard plumbing solder used on oxygen lines
components.violation: Standard plumbing solder contains tin and sometimes other contaminants that are incompatible with oxygen systems. Medical gas piping must be brazed with BCuP-5 (silver-phosphorus-copper) alloy while purging with oil-free nitrogen.

components.name: No pressure test with oil-free nitrogen before use
components.violation: Medical gas piping must undergo a standing pressure test at 1.5 times the working pressure using oil-free nitrogen for 24 hours, followed by purging and cross-connection verification before any patient use.

components.name: Alarm panels connected to building management

explanation: Medical gas piping is a specialized plumbing discipline governed by NFPA 99 and ASSE standards. Material selection, brazing procedures, and testing protocols are extremely strict because patient lives depend on system purity and integrity.

title: Grease Interceptor Installation

components.name: Interceptor sized per fixture unit load
Flow control fitting installed at inlet
Garbage disposal connected upstream of interceptor
components.violation: Garbage disposals (food waste disposers) are generally prohibited from discharging through a grease interceptor. The ground food particles overwhelm the interceptor's capacity and require much more frequent cleaning, reducing effectiveness.

components.name: Cleanout access at inlet and outlet
No sampling port on outlet
components.violation: A sampling tee or port must be installed on the outlet (downstream) side of the interceptor to allow the municipality or authority to test effluent quality. This is required by most jurisdictions and sewer use ordinances.

components.name: Interceptor properly vented

explanation: Grease interceptor installations must comply with both plumbing codes and local sewer authority requirements. Proper sizing, no garbage disposal connections, and downstream sampling are critical for commercial kitchen compliance.

title: Union Hall Training: Backflow Prevention Assembly

components.name: Isolation valves installed on both sides of the RPZ assembly
RPZ backflow preventer installed in a below-grade pit below the flood rim level
components.violation: An RPZ assembly must never be installed in a pit or vault below the flood rim level. The relief port is designed to discharge water to atmosphere when backpressure is detected. If submerged, the relief port cannot function and the device cannot protect the potable supply — defeating its entire purpose.
components.codeReference: ASSE 5013 / USC Manual of Cross-Connection Control

components.name: Test cocks obstructed and not accessible for annual testing
components.violation: Test cocks on backflow preventers must remain accessible for annual field testing by a certified tester. Obstructing or burying test cocks prevents required testing and is a violation of the water purveyor's cross-connection control program.

components.name: Assembly supported at proper height with strut channel and hangers
Relief port discharge pipe terminated with no air gap above nearest drain
components.violation: The relief port discharge must terminate with an air gap above any drain receptor. Without an air gap, a cross-connection is created at the very device meant to prevent cross-connections. The discharge must also be visible to indicate when the relief valve is operating.

components.name: Strainer installed upstream of the RPZ assembly

explanation: Backflow preventer installation is a core UA (United Association) training topic. RPZ assemblies are the highest level of backflow protection and must be installed above grade where the relief port can discharge freely. Test cocks must remain accessible for the annual certification test required by water purveyors. These are critical public health protection devices.

title: Union Hall Training: Medical Gas Brazing

components.name: Tube cut square with a tubing cutter and deburred
Joint surfaces properly cleaned and prepared before brazing
Using standard silver solder (BAg) instead of BCuP-5 on oxygen lines
components.violation: Standard silver brazing alloy (BAg series) contains flux and materials not suitable for oxygen service. Oxygen lines require phosphorus-copper-silver alloy (BCuP-5) applied without flux, as flux residue can contaminate the oxygen supply and is a combustion risk in an oxygen-enriched environment.

components.name: No nitrogen purge flowing through the pipe during brazing
components.violation: An oil-free nitrogen purge must flow continuously through the tube during all brazing operations. Without a nitrogen purge, copper oxide scale forms inside the joint. This scale can break loose and contaminate the medical gas system, potentially reaching patients and causing serious harm.

components.name: No fire watch posted during brazing operations
components.violation: A dedicated fire watch must be posted during and for a minimum period after all brazing and hot work operations per NFPA 51B. In a healthcare facility, this is especially critical due to the presence of oxygen-enriched environments and vulnerable patients.

components.name: Brazer holds current ASSE 6010 medical gas certification

explanation: Medical gas brazing is a specialized UA skill requiring ASSE 6010 certification. Apprentices learn at the union hall that oxygen lines demand phosphorus-free brazing alloy (BCuP-5), continuous nitrogen purging to prevent internal oxidation, and strict fire watch protocols. A contaminated medical gas system can directly harm or kill patients — there is zero tolerance for procedural shortcuts.

title: Butterfly Valve Installation

components.name: Butterfly valve properly aligned between flanges
8-inch butterfly valve with lever handle operator
components.violation: Butterfly valves 6 inches and larger must be equipped with a gear operator, not a lever handle. Lever handles on large valves allow rapid closure that causes water hammer, and they cannot provide the fine control needed for balancing. Gear operators also prevent unauthorized operation.

components.name: Valve stem oriented correctly per manufacturer
Flange bolts torqued to specification
Position indicator visible on valve
Isolation valve accessible for maintenance

explanation: Large butterfly valves require gear operators for controlled operation. A lever handle on an 8-inch valve allows a single person to slam the valve shut, creating destructive water hammer that can damage piping, fittings, and equipment throughout the system.

title: Valve Pressure Rating Mismatch

components.name: System designed for 125 psi operating pressure
Gate valve rated at only 100 psi installed on 125 psi system
components.violation: Valves must be rated to exceed the maximum operating pressure by at least 50% per University specifications. A 100 psi rated valve on a 125 psi system is under-rated and risks catastrophic valve body failure. A minimum 200 psi rated valve (Class 150) is required.

components.name: Pressure relief valve set at 150 psi
Pressure gauge installed with snubber
Check valve on pump discharge
Pipe supports at proper intervals
System hydrostatic test completed and documented

explanation: Valve pressure ratings must exceed maximum operating pressure by a safety margin. University specs require a minimum 50% safety factor. Installing under-rated valves creates a risk of catastrophic failure that can flood occupied spaces and endanger personnel.

title: Grooved Coupling Gasket on Steam System

components.name: Grooved-end pipe properly roll-grooved
Grooved coupling with EPDM gasket installed on 300°F steam line
components.violation: EPDM gaskets have a maximum continuous service temperature of 250°F. Using EPDM on a 300°F steam line exceeds the gasket temperature rating, leading to gasket degradation, steam leaks, and potential burn injuries. A Grade T or high-temperature silicone gasket rated for the service temperature is required.
components.codeReference: ASTM D2000 / Victaulic Grade T Gasket Specifications

components.name: Coupling housing torqued to manufacturer specifications
Pipe properly aligned and supported at coupling
Steam trap downstream of equipment
Drip leg at end of steam main
Safety relief valve protecting isolated section

explanation: Gasket material compatibility with service temperature is critical in steam systems. EPDM is excellent for hot water up to 250°F but will fail rapidly at steam temperatures above its rating. Always verify gasket material ratings match the actual service conditions before installation.

title: High-Rise Office Lavatory Rough-In (high-rise office Level 31)

components.name: Non-residential lavatory faucet rated at 0.5 GPM @ 60 PSI
Water closet rated at 1.28 gallons per flush (ASME A112.19.2)
Type M copper supply piping above floor
components.violation: high-rise office specifications require Type L copper for above-floor domestic water piping. Type M has thinner walls and is not rated for the higher pressures found in high-rise buildings. Type K is required for underground installations.

components.name: P-trap with proper trap arm to vent
Cast iron soil pipe with Husky 4000 no-hub couplings
Waste pipe at 2% slope for 4-inch line
components.violation: A 4-inch waste pipe requires a minimum slope of 1% (1/8 inch per foot), not 2%. The 2% slope is the requirement for 2-inch pipe. Over-sloping a 4-inch pipe can cause liquids to outrun solids, leaving debris behind in the pipe.

components.name: Water hammer arresters (J.R. Smith Fig. 5005) at fixture groups

explanation: High-rise commercial office TI plumbing requires correct pipe materials and slopes. Type L copper is the minimum for above-floor supply piping in commercial high-rises due to higher system pressures, and waste pipe slopes must match the pipe diameter — 2% for 2-inch and 1% for 4-inch.

title: Office Breakroom Sink Installation (high-rise office Level 30)

components.name: Kohler K-3822 pantry sink properly mounted
Everpure 4FC5 water filtration system connected
Kitchen faucet set to 2.2 GPM as default flow rate
components.violation: Per CPC 2022, kitchen faucets must default to 1.8 GPM maximum. The temporary increase to 2.2 GPM is permitted only for pot-filling or similar tasks and must require manual activation. The default setting must be 1.8 GPM or less.

components.name: 1-1/2" P-trap on sink drain
Pipe sleeve through masonry wall using 20 gauge sheet metal
components.violation: Pipe sleeves through masonry walls require Schedule 40 galvanized steel pipe. The 20 gauge sheet metal sleeves are only permitted through framed (stud) partitions. Masonry penetrations need the structural rigidity of Schedule 40 steel.

components.name: Supply valves with proper shutoff access

explanation: Commercial breakroom plumbing in California must comply with CPC 2022 water conservation requirements. Kitchen faucets must default to 1.8 GPM, and pipe sleeves must match the wall construction type — Schedule 40 galvanized for masonry, 20 gauge sheet metal for framed walls.

title: Water Heater Room — Leak Detection (high-rise office Level 32)

components.name: Commercial water heater with T&P relief valve
Solenoid valve on supply line with leak detector pad
Pipe penetration through breakroom wall sealed with silicone caulk only
components.violation: Kitchen and breakroom pipe penetrations require Link-Seal Model OS-316 modular seals. Silicone caulk alone does not provide the code-required waterproof and gastight seal at penetrations. Link-Seals provide a mechanical compression seal that remains serviceable and can accommodate pipe movement.

components.name: Expansion tank on cold water supply
Annular space between pipe and sleeve measures 3/4 inch
components.violation: The maximum annular space between the pipe and the sleeve is 1/2 inch per specification. A 3/4-inch gap is too large to properly firestop and seal. The tighter tolerance ensures the firestopping material (mineral wool plus non-hardening compound) can effectively fill the annular space.

components.name: Seismic strapping on water heater per CBC
Firestopping with mineral wool and Duxseal at rated penetrations

explanation: High-rise commercial office TI work requires specific penetration sealing methods. Link-Seal OS-316 modular seals are specified for kitchen/breakroom penetrations, and the annular space between pipes and sleeves must not exceed 1/2 inch to ensure proper firestopping performance.

title: CRAC Unit Condensate Drain (high-rise office Level 31)

components.name: CRAC unit condensate collected in drain pan
3/4-inch condensate drain (CD) piped down from unit
Condensate drain connected directly to sanitary sewer with no air gap
components.violation: Condensate drains must discharge through an indirect waste connection. The high-rise office spec requires the condensate to spill to a lavatory tailpiece, providing a visible air gap. A direct connection to the sanitary system could allow sewer gases to back up into the CRAC unit and the server room.

components.name: Condensate piping properly insulated to prevent sweating
Trap primer connection on floor drain near CRAC
Urinal in adjacent restroom rated at 0.5 gallons per flush
components.violation: high-rise office specifications call for 0.125 gallon per flush urinals (pint-flush). A 0.5 GPF urinal uses four times the water required and does not meet California water conservation requirements or the project's LEED enhanced commissioning targets.

explanation: CRAC unit condensate must be handled as indirect waste — never directly connected to the sewer. The high-rise commercial office specifies spilling condensate to a lavatory tailpiece for visibility and air gap protection. Ultra-low-flow fixtures (0.125 GPF urinals) are specified for LEED compliance.

title: High-Rise Firestopping and Penetration Seals (high-rise office Levels 30-32)

components.name: Cast iron soil pipe through rated floor assembly with Husky 4000 couplings
Firestopping with mineral wool packing and Duxseal non-hardening compound
Schedule 40 galvanized steel sleeve through masonry core wall
Type K copper used for above-floor domestic water riser between floors
components.violation: Type K copper is specified for underground installations only. Above-floor risers must use Type L copper per high-rise office specifications. Type K is heavier and more expensive than necessary for above-ground service. Using Type K above floor wastes material and budget without adding any performance benefit in an above-ground application.

components.name: Link-Seal OS-316 at breakroom penetration with 5/8-inch annular space
components.violation: The annular space between the pipe and sleeve must not exceed 1/2 inch. At 5/8 inch, the gap is too large for the Link-Seal to properly compress and seal. The sleeve must be resized or the pipe centered within the existing sleeve to achieve the maximum 1/2-inch annular space.

components.name: Water hammer arresters installed per J.R. Smith Fig. 5005 at each fixture group
Trap primers installed on all floor drains per CPC

explanation: High-rise tenant improvement work demands precision in pipe material selection and penetration sealing. Type L copper is for above-floor, Type K for underground. Annular space tolerances at penetrations are critical — exceeding 1/2 inch compromises firestopping and Link-Seal performance. These details are verified during LEED enhanced commissioning.
